isolated_buffer_orders.name: ascending
isolated_buffer_orders.openpyxl: representative output for every case opened

benchmark_protocol.isolated_preparation: Each matrix generated one deterministic worksheet XML payload in a separate preparation process. Ascending and descending payload SHA-256 values matched for every profile. Payload generation, ZIP inspection and openpyxl validation were outside timed benchmark processes.
benchmark_protocol.isolated_representative_policy: Measured run nearest target-entry total wall-time median; earliest run breaks ties.
benchmark_protocol.patch_verification: Every Patch matrix passed schema-v15 telemetry, bounded writer-call, staged CRC, output-plan byte accounting, raw compressed-payload and openpyxl checks. The formula representative retained the recorded external hyperlink target.
benchmark_protocol.process_priority: Windows High priority runner processes; benchmark children inherited that priority.
benchmark_protocol.timing_limit: GetProcessTimes process CPU is quantized on this host. Wall-time medians, maximum per-call wall time and process peak working set are reported separately; no ETW trace or pure zlib encoder attribution was collected.

validation.focused_tests: passed: package reader core and ZIP failures
validation.no_images_profile: build and image-disabled runtime smoke passed
validation.install_consumer_smoke: passed for production, stored-only and no-images installations

resource_lifecycle: No background worker was introduced. Benchmark payloads, XLSX outputs and install-consumer trees were kept only under build/qa for evidence capture and were removed after the committed matrix reports were hashed. No FastXLSX benchmark process remained. Package writer OVERLAPPED state remains local and joins pending I/O before return.
qualification: Claims are limited to the recorded same-machine deterministic workloads. They are not generalized to arbitrary workbooks, other machines, Office compatibility, physical cache-hit rate, pure encoder performance or full-feature superiority.
